user_query:
I need a unified reminders and follow-up list for my crypto drafts. Please work entirely in the local workspace.

Inputs to use:
- Parse YAML front matter from all Markdown files under drafts/ to extract:
  - title (string)
  - review_due (YYYY-MM-DD)
  - follow_up_with (list of names)
- From each draft body, find Markdown links and verify whether the linked files exist (resolve links relative to the draft file location). Record any missing attachments per draft.
- Parse notes/todo.md for lines that start with "REMIND YYYY-MM-DD: " and extract the date and the reminder text after the colon.
- Run the command `python3 tools/encrypt.py` with no arguments and capture its exit code, stdout, and stderr. Treat this as a tool health check; if it errors, include a single reminder entry that summarizes the error.

Deliverables to create:
1) reminders/reminders.json — an array of objects sorted by date ascending where each object has the following fields:
   - type: one of "draft", "todo", or "tool_error"
   - date: ISO date string (for drafts use review_due, for todos use the date in the REMIND line; for tool_error use the same date as in the system clock is not required — just set date to the latest date found among draft/todo items if any exist; otherwise omit the date field for the tool_error entry)
   - title: for drafts use the draft title; for todos use the text after "REMIND YYYY-MM-DD: "; for tool_error use "Encrypt tool check failed"
   - people: for drafts include the follow_up_with list; omit for other types
   - missing_attachments: for drafts include a list of resolved file paths that do not exist; for drafts with no links or all links present, include an empty list; omit for other types
   - severity: "normal" by default; use "warning" for any draft with one or more missing_attachments; use "error" for the tool_error entry
   - source_file: the path to the source file (e.g., drafts/…md or notes/todo.md; for tool_error use "tools/encrypt.py")
   - error_summary: include only for the tool_error entry; set to the last non-empty line of stderr from running `python3 tools/encrypt.py` with no arguments

2) reminders/notifications.txt — a human-readable summary where each line includes at least the date (if present), the title, the type, and the severity.

3) logs/encrypt_check.txt — a plain text log that includes:
   - The exact command run
   - The numeric exit code
   - The full captured stdout
   - The full captured stderr

Constraints and notes:
- Do not access the Internet.
- Use relative paths exactly as they appear in the Markdown links when checking attachment existence (resolve them relative to the draft file location before testing existence).
- Consider only the provided files in this workspace.
- The three required activities are essential: a) read local files and extract the requested fields and REMIND lines, b) inspect the attachments directory structure to confirm presence/absence of linked files, and c) analyze the command’s output/error to produce the tool_error reminder and the detailed log.
- Output paths must be created exactly as specified: reminders/reminders.json, reminders/notifications.txt, logs/encrypt_check.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
